text: Franz Beckenbauer believes Jurgen Klopp would be a suitable replacement for Pep Guardiola at Bayern Munich when Guardiola's contract ends in 2016.  Although Guardiola is content in Munich and likely to stay, Beckenbauer sees Klopp as a strong candidate due to his past success in the Bundesliga and his perceived fit for Bayern's style. Beckenbauer also believes that for Bayern to overcome Barcelona in the Champions League, they will need a fully healthy squad, particularly key players like Franck Ribery and Arjen Robben, to neutralize Lionel Messi's impact.

Esteban Cambiasso, a seasoned midfielder with experience winning the Champions League and multiple Serie A titles, believes saving Leicester City from relegation would be a career highlight.  Despite Leicester's current struggles at the bottom of the Premier League table, Cambiasso remains optimistic, stating that the team has a chance to win matches and secure their place in the league.  He acknowledges the difficult task ahead but is confident in his teammates' abilities to overcome the challenge.  Cambiasso joined Leicester last summer and is determined to help the newly promoted club avoid an immediate return to the Championship.

Jonathan Trott made a highly anticipated return to Test cricket after a 17-month absence due to a stress-related illness, but his comeback was short-lived.  He was dismissed for a duck in the first over, caught by Darren Bravo off the bowling of Jerome Taylor.  Trott, normally a middle-order batsman, had been promoted to open the innings but failed to capitalize on the opportunity.  This early setback left England struggling at 1-1 after just five balls in the first Test against the West Indies in Antigua.

A 12-year-old girl named Shannon Hayes believes one of her pet hens has laid the smallest chicken egg in the world, measuring just 1.8 centimeters long.  This tiny egg, smaller than a five pence piece, could potentially beat the current record of 2.0 centimeters. Shannon discovered the tiny egg in her hen coop and is carefully storing it in the refrigerator until she can apply for a Guinness World Record. If successful, this would be the third time the record for the smallest chicken egg has been broken in recent years.

Six Leeds players, five signed by banned president Massimo Cellino and one by his associate, withdrew from Saturday's game against Charlton citing injury.  Coach Neil Redfearn and the medical team were reportedly unaware of these injuries, raising suspicions about their legitimacy.  The players, including key starters like Antenucci and Silvestri, claimed they sustained the injuries during training or a previous match, but fans and pundits are questioning their claims.  This incident further destabilizes Leeds United, already struggling with Redfearn's uncertain future and Cellino's impending return after a ban.

Nicola Sturgeon, the leader of the SNP, has undergone a significant style transformation, ditching her former boxy jackets and severe suits for a more glamorous and modern look.  She has embraced feminine dresses, cinched waist skirts, and a sleeker hairstyle, earning praise for her recent sartorial choices. This change, rumored to be aided by a personal shopper and stylist, has been particularly noticeable since the 2007 Scottish parliament election.  Sturgeon's new image is a stark contrast to her earlier appearances, which often featured shapeless clothing and a choppy hairstyle.

John R. Lind, a 34-year-old hardware store worker, pleaded guilty to contaminating his female colleague's coffee with his semen in an attempt to gain her attention. Lind also admitted to masturbating over her desk on multiple occasions while she was absent. Maahs, the victim, initially thought her coffee smelled bad due to spoiled milk but later discovered the truth. This incident prompted Maahs to propose legislation making such acts a felony.

Jamie Carragher believes that Chelsea captain John Terry is the best defender to have ever played in the Premier League, citing his outstanding performance in the recent draw against Arsenal as evidence.  Carragher emphasizes Terry's influence on Chelsea's defensive prowess and highlights his consistency over the years.  Chelsea manager Jose Mourinho also praised Terry, calling his performance against Arsenal the best he's ever seen from the defender. Terry's leadership and defensive skill have been crucial to Chelsea's pursuit of their first Premier League title since 2010.

Police investigating the suspected murder of Deborah Steel, a landlady who disappeared in 1997, recently dug up the patio of her former pub, the Royal Standard, but found no clues.  The investigation has been reclassified as a murder inquiry, and a 73-year-old man is currently on bail. Deborah's family continues to seek answers about her disappearance, hoping to finally lay her to rest. Anyone with information is urged to contact Cambridgeshire Police.

Manny Pacquiao, the underdog in the upcoming $300 million fight against Floyd Mayweather, believes he must fight with the ferocity of a warrior to secure victory on May 2nd. Pacquiao, promising a thrilling fight worth the years of anticipation, insists he understands what it takes to defeat Mayweather and will leave no doubt about his victory. He intends to capitalize on any opportunity to knock out Mayweather,  believing it is time for the undefeated champion to experience defeat at his hands. The fight, billed as the biggest sporting event in history, will take place at the MGM Grand in Las Vegas.

A police horse named Charly was vandalized with graffiti while on patrol in Venice Beach, California. The perpetrator spray-painted the tag "RBS" on the horse's flank, and authorities hope to identify the individual through this tag.  Although the paint was easily removed, the LAPD is seeking public assistance in finding the person responsible for the act of vandalism.  Locals expressed their disapproval of the act, calling it cruel and disrespectful.

A 13-year-old boy in Medina, Ohio, stole $25,000 from his grandfather and distributed it to his classmates and acquaintances. The boy's actions were discovered by teachers at Claggett Middle School, leading to an investigation by Medina police.  Officers have recovered $7,000 of the stolen money and some of the items purchased with it, and charges are pending against those involved. The school is cooperating with law enforcement in the investigation.

Bastia president Pierre-Marie Geronimi has demanded the resignation of French League president Frederic Thiriez after he refused to shake the players' hands before the League Cup final. Geronimi argues that Thiriez's actions are unacceptable and that he is unfit to lead a league with 41 clubs. This is not the first time Thiriez has been criticized by Bastia, as he also did not hand them their Ligue 2 champions' trophy in 2012.  Paris Saint Germain defeated Bastia 4-0 in the final, claiming their fifth League Cup title.

Hayleigh Mcbay, a 17-year-old from Scotland, jokingly pretended to break up with her boyfriend David Clarke on April Fools' Day via WhatsApp.  David, in a playful twist, pretended to reciprocate her sentiments, leading to a shocking but humorous exchange.  Hayleigh shared the conversation online, which quickly went viral, garnering both positive reactions and hateful trolling.  Despite the negative comments, Hayleigh clarified that the exchange was unplanned and expressed her reluctance to joke about such sensitive topics in the future.

David Wihby, a senior aide to US Senator Kelly Ayotte, resigned after his arrest on a misdemeanor charge of soliciting prostitution in Nashua, New Hampshire. Wihby, who served as Ayotte's state director and had held other elected positions, was one of ten men arrested in a police sting operation targeting online prostitution advertisements. Ayotte expressed shock and sadness over the incident, stating that Wihby was unable to continue his duties. Wihby is scheduled to be arraigned in early May.

Arsene Wenger meticulously selects his Arsenal starting lineup, agonizing over choices for the entire week.  While he typically has eight or nine players in mind the day before the match, he considers factors like recent form, experience, and balance to make his final decision.  Wenger consults with medical staff on the morning of the game to confirm player fitness before locking in his starting XI. Despite waiting until the last minute to officially confirm his team, Wenger actively analyzes and reconsiders potential lineups throughout the entire week.

On a recent episode of Jeopardy, contestant Tom gave a highly inappropriate answer to a question about puberty. When asked about the presumed age of adulthood signaling puberty, Tom responded with "What is the age of consent?", a significantly different and disturbingly suggestive answer.  This sparked outrage and confusion among viewers who took to Twitter to express their shock and disapproval, with #jeopardy trending due to the controversial moment. The correct answer, of course, was "What is puberty?".

Rafael Nadal began his clay court season with a dominant 6-2, 6-1 victory over Lucas Pouille in the Monte Carlo Masters.  The 21-time Grand Slam champion displayed his trademark consistency, making only five unforced errors and breaking Pouille's serve twice in each set.  Although Nadal acknowledged he's not the favorite to win the tournament, this victory provides a crucial confidence boost after recent disappointing results.  He will face either John Isner or Viktor Troicki in the next round.

An annual fishing event in Auckland, New Zealand, saw participants using homemade boats made from unexpected materials like furniture and even a car.  The event, which attracted the attention of coastguards due to its unusual nature, involved vessels like a couch strapped to tires and a floating Subaru.  While one vessel capsized, no one was injured, and the coastguard commended participants for wearing life jackets.  Despite some reported alcohol consumption and a lighthearted, even potentially dangerous, atmosphere, the event's organizers emphasized safety and community positivity.

Navinder Sarao, a British trader, is accused by US prosecutors of triggering the 2010 "Flash Crash" on Wall Street, netting himself £27 million in profits.  Evidence presented in court includes an email from Sarao boasting about his ability to earn £1 million in a week during volatile market conditions. Sarao was arrested and appeared in court, but remains in custody despite being granted bail due to an unfulfilled surety payment. He faces potential trial in the Northern District Court of Illinois.

The official ad for the highly anticipated Floyd Mayweather vs. Manny Pacquiao fight, set for May 2nd in Las Vegas, has been released, generating immense hype. The ad, featuring the two boxing legends facing off in a desert setting, emphasizes the historical significance of the bout,  dubbed "Two legends. One destiny."  The fight is projected to shatter pay-per-view records, potentially surpassing Mayweather's previous fights with Canelo Alvarez and Oscar De La Hoya in viewership and revenue. Fans in the US will pay up to $100 to watch the event, while UK viewers can access the fight for £19.95 through Sky.

Rory McIlroy took a break from golf to compete in a match of Circular Soccer against actor Jamie Dornan. McIlroy emerged victorious, defeating Dornan 2-1 in the first Circular Soccer Showdown of 2015.  Circular Soccer, a new football concept designed to improve skill and ball control, has been gaining traction with FIFA integrating it into their youth programs and Manchester United testing it at their academy.  With McIlroy's participation, the sport may soon become more widely played.

Colleen Ann Harris, nicknamed "Grandma Cokie", has been found guilty of first-degree murder for the 2013 shooting death of her third husband, Robert Harris. This conviction comes after she was acquitted in 1985 for the shotgun killing of her second husband, James Batten, where self-defense was argued. Harris, now 73, claimed memory loss regarding the events leading up to Robert Harris' death but was found guilty after a short deliberation by the jury. She faces a potential sentence of 50 years to life.

Floyd Mayweather and Manny Pacquiao, the two top boxers in the world, are spending time with their families ahead of their upcoming historic fight. Pacquiao is in Los Angeles with his wife Jinkee and their five children, while Mayweather is in Las Vegas with his girlfriend Doralie Medina.  Both boxers are expected to start their final week of intense training this week before tapering down for the fight.  They will both need to weigh in at no more than 154 pounds this weekend and then again at the official weigh-in a week before the fight.

Martin Guptill, who recently excelled in the Cricket World Cup, has been recalled to the New Zealand Test squad for their upcoming series against England.  Matt Henry, a promising paceman, receives his first Test call-up despite limited first-class experience. Coach Mike Hesson emphasizes the stability of the Test squad while acknowledging opportunities for emerging players.  Both the Test and limited-overs squads feature several familiar faces, with Mitchell Santner the only uncapped player in the ODI and T20 teams.

During a postgame press conference after Kentucky's loss to Wisconsin, guard Andrew Harrison uttered a racial slur towards Wisconsin player Frank Kaminsky, despite covering his mouth. Harrison later apologized to Kaminsky, who has stated that the incident is over and nothing needs to be made of it. The offensive comment, caught on a live microphone, caused immediate backlash on social media and prompted an investigation by the University of Kentucky.  Despite the controversy, Harrison and his twin brother Aaron remain projected NBA draft picks.

Scottish comedian Limmy shocked his fans by posting a video of himself with a large spider crawling out of his mouth and across his face. The comedian, known for his absurdist humor, captioned the seven-second clip "Sweet dreams," describing it as a self-imposed "bushtucker trial." While some fans were repulsed, others found the video hilarious and lauded it as one of the internet's best creations.  The spider appeared unharmed by the experience.

Andras Janos Vass, a 25-year-old Hungarian man, was convicted of human trafficking and racketeering for luring young, gay Eastern European men to the United States under false pretenses. Vass promised them jobs and high wages but instead took their travel documents, threatened their families, and forced them into sex slavery in New York City and Miami. Three of Vass's victims testified against him, revealing he used the gay dating app Planet Romeo to lure them, keeping them captive and forcing them into 20-hour days of sex work. Vass faces a maximum sentence of 155 years in prison, with a minimum of 21 years.

Beijing International Airport, the second busiest in the world, is constructing the world's largest terminal, Terminal 1, to accommodate the surge in Chinese tourists. Designed by renowned architect Zaha Hadid, the 700,000 square meter terminal will handle 45 million passengers annually upon its 2018 opening. The terminal's innovative six-tier design prioritizes passenger comfort by minimizing walking distances and maximizing connectivity through a central courtyard. This massive expansion reflects the dramatic growth of outbound Chinese tourism, which saw 109 million travelers spending $164 billion in 2014.

The Bondi Icebergs Club pool was temporarily drained for a unique fashion show as part of Mercedes-Benz Fashion Week Australia.  The show, presented by the lifestyle brand Ten Pieces, featured a monochrome capsule collection designed to be worn in 20 different ways.  Founded by Icebergs restaurateur Maurice Terzini and his partner Lucy Hinckfuss, Ten Pieces showcased its androgynous designs against the backdrop of the ocean, with guests enjoying the sunny weather and refreshments. The unusual location drew attention from both beachgoers and passersby, creating a memorable moment at the iconic Bondi Beach.

Alain Robert, known as the "French Spiderman," scaled Dubai's Cayan Tower, a 1,007ft skyscraper, using only sticky tape and chalk.  The 52-year-old free-climber completed the climb in 90 minutes, captivating hundreds of spectators who watched him ascend the 75-story building without a safety harness.  This daring feat, which was captured on video, marks another successful climb for Robert, who has a history of scaling famous buildings around the world, sometimes resulting in legal trouble for unauthorized ascents.  Despite his history of falls, Robert continued to push his limits, even climbing Dubai's Burj Khalifa in 2011, though he used a rope and harness for that climb.

Stuart Pearce believes Manchester City manager Manuel Pellegrini deserves more time to improve the team's performance, despite their recent struggles and fall from title contention. While acknowledging the need for squad changes, Pearce argues against hastily replacing managers, hoping Pellegrini will remain at the helm. He attributes City's decline partly to Yaya Toure's diminished impact this season.  Overall, Pearce believes City should focus on strengthening their squad and giving Pellegrini the opportunity to turn things around.

A married couple, Derek Lowe (38) and Tina Lowe (33), were tragically killed after being struck by an Amtrak train in Durham, North Carolina on Easter Sunday. The couple was reportedly living in a homeless camp and may have been on the tracks collecting scrap metal. The train, carrying 166 passengers, was delayed for nearly three hours before continuing its journey to New York City.  No passengers were injured in the incident, except for one who fell ill during the delay and received medical attention.

Paul Gregory, 51, was fined for fraudulently obtaining a blue disability badge by falsely claiming he could only walk 200 meters.  Council officials discovered his deception after reading a newspaper article detailing his participation in a 191-mile hike and other walking trips. Gregory pleaded guilty to two counts of dishonestly obtaining a blue badge and was sentenced to a fine, unpaid work, and a supervision order. Bedford Borough Council condemned his actions as "one of the most outrageous cases" of blue badge fraud they've investigated.

Manchester United's interest in Tottenham Hotspur goalkeeper Hugo Lloris faces a significant hurdle: Spurs chairman Daniel Levy's tough negotiating tactics and a hefty £35 million price tag. Levy, known for extracting maximum value from his players, is determined to keep Lloris at the club and is unwilling to sell him to a domestic rival.  This high price tag and Levy's stance have already led to Manchester United showing hesitation about pursuing a summer move for Lloris. Lloris, considered one of the Premier League's best goalkeepers, signed a five-year contract with Spurs last summer, further strengthening their resolve to keep him.

The IRS struggled to serve taxpayers this filing season, with over 8 million calls disconnected and only 40% of callers reaching a representative. IRS Commissioner John Koskinen attributed the severe service issues to budget cuts, citing a $1.2 billion reduction since 2010.  Koskinen stated that these cuts were necessary because the IRS was legally obligated to fund the implementation of the Affordable Care Act, leaving them with limited options for allocating resources. He emphasized the agency's need for additional funding to improve its outdated systems and provide adequate customer service.

Jeremy Clarkson, recently sacked from the BBC, made a public appearance at a charity auction in Chipping Norton, joking about his past restrictions on swearing and his current job search. He entertained the audience with his trademark humor, including a humorous blunder mistaking a male bidder for a female. This marked Clarkson's first public appearance since his controversial departure from the BBC following an alleged assault on a producer.  His appearance comes just weeks after he pulled out of hosting "Have I Got News for You," further highlighting his current situation.

Chilean pickpocketing gangs have become the most effective thieves operating in central London, targeting wealthy tourists with intelligence-led operations.  These gangs, often posing as families or businessmen, blend seamlessly into crowds and strike with precision, stealing cash, valuables, and electronics from hotels and busy shopping areas.  While other nationalities like Romanians and Bulgarians are also involved in pickpocketing, Chilean gangs are particularly adept at evading detection, moving across Europe with short-term VISAs to avoid consistent police scrutiny.  London police are actively combating this organized crime by monitoring CCTV, increasing patrols, and collaborating with international law enforcement agencies to disrupt these gangs.

Two children died in an accidental vehicle fire at a Las Vegas apartment complex. Four children were playing in and around a parked minivan when one accidentally ignited a fire inside. Two children escaped unharmed, but two others were trapped and found dead after the fire was extinguished. Police do not expect criminal charges because the child who started the fire is believed to be too young.

Real Madrid received a passionate welcome from their fans as they arrived at the Santiago Bernabeu for their Champions League quarter-final against Atletico Madrid.  The electric atmosphere spurred the team on, but Atletico proved a tough opponent until Javier Hernandez scored the winning goal in the 88th minute, securing a 1-0 aggregate victory. Real Madrid now await their semi-final opponents, which could be Barcelona, Juventus, or Bayern Munich.  This triumph was celebrated with a video by defender Raphael Varane, capturing the fervent support from the stands.

NBC's Today show made a blunder by misidentifying Lorne Michaels, creator of Saturday Night Live, as "Lauren" Michaels during a segment on TIME magazine's 100 most influential people. The mistake, made on the East Coast feed, was corrected in other broadcasts and online. Michaels, a prominent figure in comedy and a legend at NBC, was being interviewed for his inclusion on the prestigious list, which also featured figures like Kanye West and Kim Kardashian. Despite the embarrassing error, Michaels continues to be recognized for his influential role in comedy, earning a spot on both TIME's and Billboard's power lists.

The roof of the Korean Union United Methodist Church in Rahway, New Jersey, collapsed during an Easter Sunday service, injuring 14 people.  The collapse occurred while approximately 75 churchgoers were present, sending debris raining down and causing panic. Thirteen people sustained minor injuries, while one woman suffered a serious head injury.  Authorities are investigating the cause of the collapse and have deemed the building unsafe until an engineering firm can assess its stability.

German Chancellor Angela Merkel spent her Easter holiday on the Italian island of Ischia with her husband, Joachim Sauer. Despite the unseasonably cool weather, the couple enjoyed the luxurious amenities of their five-star hotel, including thermal baths and Pilates classes. Merkel reportedly hired a private Pilates instructor and indulged in healthy vegetarian meals.  Sauer, as usual, kept a low profile, rarely appearing alongside his wife.

Manchester United striker Radamel Falcao has identified Sergio Aguero as the biggest threat in Sunday's Manchester derby. Falcao, who missed the earlier encounter this season, acknowledges Aguero's history of scoring against United and emphasizes the need to closely monitor him. Despite playing a limited role under Louis van Gaal, Falcao expresses happiness at United and his willingness to be patient.  The Colombian striker understands the significance of the derby for both sets of fans and anticipates a phenomenal atmosphere at the Etihad Stadium.

Andy Murray enjoyed a double victory in Barcelona, witnessing Lionel Messi and Barcelona defeat PSG in the Champions League quarter-final and watching them win against Valencia the previous week. Murray, who is training on clay courts in Barcelona with Jonas Bjorkman, attended both matches with his best man, Ross Hutchins. This trip to Barcelona marked a successful month for Murray, who recently married his childhood sweetheart, Kim Sears. Murray will compete in the BMW Open in Munich later this month before returning to Spain for the Madrid Masters in May.

Martina Navratilova's brief coaching partnership with Agnieszka Radwanska has ended after just a few months. The decision was mutual, as Navratilova couldn't fully commit to the role due to other commitments. Radwanska, who has struggled this season with a 13-11 record, expressed gratitude for Navratilova's expertise and acknowledged the difficulty of a long-term partnership with limited availability.  The split comes ahead of the French Open, which begins on May 24th.

On April 23rd, St. George's Day, numerous English footballers both past and present took to social media to express their pride.  Harry Kane shared a childhood photo, Rio Ferdinand reminisced about an iconic England goal, and Gary Cahill participated in a video celebrating what it means to represent England. Other players like Nathaniel Clyne, Charlie Austin, and Peter Crouch also joined in the celebrations with social media posts.

Cristiano Ronaldo has reached a significant milestone by scoring his 300th goal for Real Madrid in just 288 games.  To celebrate this achievement, club president Florentino Perez presented Ronaldo with a special commemorative jersey.  Ronaldo is currently third on Real Madrid's all-time scoring list, trailing only Raul and Alfredo di Stefano.  Despite this achievement, Ronaldo will be missing his teammate Karim Benzema, who suffered a knee injury and will be unavailable for Saturday's match against Malaga.

Fernando Hierro, a Real Madrid legend and assistant coach, believes the club can win a second consecutive Champions League title. He cites their strength, confidence, and recent victory in a close final as evidence of their capabilities.  Hierro emphasizes that the desire to win the Champions League is ingrained in Real Madrid's DNA and that the current squad can achieve a similar success to the teams of the late 1990s and early 2000s. Despite the challenge of being the first team in history to win back-to-back Champions Leagues, Hierro remains optimistic about their chances.

Chelsea are the frontrunners to sign Brazilian talent Kenedy from Fluminense.  The club has an advantage as Kenedy's club has agreed to sell his rights to an agency that also represents Chelsea players Oscar, Willian, and Ramires.  Furthermore, Kenedy's agent is former Chelsea player Deco, and despite interest from other top clubs like Manchester United and Barcelona, Chelsea is expected to secure his signature. The 19-year-old has represented Brazil at both the U17 and U20 levels and is considered highly promising.

Mario Mandzukic, Atletico Madrid's striker, endured a rough physical encounter with Real Madrid players in the Champions League quarter-final first leg, sustaining a nose cut and a stomach punch. Despite these injuries and missing Saturday's La Liga match, Mandzukic is expected to be fit for the return leg at the Bernabeu on Wednesday.  Atletico coach Diego Simeone has noted Mandzukic's progress in training and will likely incorporate him into his starting lineup alongside Antoine Griezmann, who is in excellent form with 20 goals in La Liga.

Italian authorities launched a major anti-terrorism operation targeting an al Qaeda-linked cell suspected of plotting attacks in Italy, Afghanistan, and Pakistan.  Wiretaps revealed the group had direct contact with Osama bin Laden before his death and discussed targeting the Vatican. The suspects, many from Pakistan, were under surveillance for years and some were involved in a deadly 2009 bombing in Pakistan.  The operation resulted in raids across seven Italian provinces and the expected arrest of 18 individuals.

Real Madrid is heavily pursuing Manchester United goalkeeper David De Gea but is willing to wait until next summer if United refuses to negotiate a deal.  If they can't secure De Gea this summer, Real Madrid is considering signing Chelsea's Petr Cech as a temporary solution. Meanwhile, Real Madrid star James Rodriguez is set to return from injury and start against Celta Vigo on Sunday.  Elsewhere in the football world, Barcelona star Lionel Messi is recovering from an injury and is expected to play against Celta Vigo.

Ben Flower, making his first appearance since a six-month suspension for punching an opponent in the Grand Final, was surprised and humbled by the standing ovation he received from Wigan fans. While acknowledging the fans' support, Flower believes he doesn't deserve such praise due to his actions last season.  He aims to regain his aggressive playing style but emphasizes that aggression doesn't equate to being a dirty player and that he needs to learn better emotional management.  Flower views his past mistake as a massive one and is determined to move forward.

Sandie Konopelski, a 58-year-old wildlife-rescue volunteer, was tragically killed by a MetroLink train in Illinois after responding to a call about an opossum on the tracks. Konopelski, licensed by the Illinois Department of Natural Resources, was known for her dedication to animal welfare and had been involved with the Bi-State Wildlife Hotline for many years. Authorities are unsure why she did not hear the approaching train and were unable to avoid the collision, which resulted in a two-hour delay in train service. Konopelski's death has deeply saddened the local community and the wildlife rescue organization she was a part of.

Kevin Rebbie, a 56-year-old man from Pennsylvania, was arrested for sexually abusing a 15-year-old girl over a four-year period and filming her and other individuals in their bathrooms using a hidden camera. The investigation began when the girl discovered the camera and reported Rebbie's actions, including molestation from the ages of nine to thirteen.  Authorities found 41 videos on Rebbie's computer, some depicting explicit images of minors, which he admitted to recording. Rebbie faces multiple charges, including aggravated indecent assault and invasion of privacy, and is currently being held on a $500,000 bail.

Aaronessa Keaton, a 24-year-old woman from Phoenix, Arizona, was arrested and charged with manslaughter for a 2014 crash that resulted in the death of a 5-year-old child.  While eight months pregnant and under the influence of marijuana and benzodiazepines, Keaton's vehicle collided head-on with a car carrying two children.  After her arrest, Keaton showed no remorse, reportedly stating "Sh*t happens."  The other child in the car survived, as did Keaton's baby, who was delivered prematurely via emergency C-section and also tested positive for drugs.

Greg Dyke, chairman of the Football Association, has returned a £16,000 Parmigiani watch he received as a gift from the Brazilian FA during the World Cup.  FIFA's ethics committee had threatened sanctions if Dyke did not return the watch, which was part of a larger distribution of expensive gifts that were later recalled. Dyke initially refused to return the watch, intending to auction it for charity, but ultimately relented after six months of legal wrangling.  The watches will now be auctioned for a Brazilian charity, and Dyke plans to make a personal donation to Breast Cancer Care.

Hull City are interested in signing QPR defender Nedum Onuoha, who could be available as the club looks to cut costs if they are relegated.  Onuoha, who recently played a key role in QPR's win over West Brom, has also attracted attention from Everton, West Ham, and Stoke City. Hull are also reportedly interested in West Brom striker Victor Anichebe, who is expected to leave the club this summer. QPR, meanwhile, are considering bringing in Catania defender Nicolas Spolli as a potential replacement for Onuoha.

"Kid Kulafu," a new film about Manny Pacquiao, portrays his journey from poverty to boxing stardom. The film, based on Pacquiao's own life, highlights his courage and resilience in overcoming challenges and achieving success. Director Paul Soriano emphasizes Pacquiao's humility and hard work, stating that he "was born with nothing but because of God, because of hard work and faith, he became something."  The film's release coincides with Pacquiao's highly anticipated fight against Floyd Mayweather Jr. next month, a bout expected to generate over $300 million.

Floyd Mayweather Jr. is continuing his rigorous training for the highly anticipated mega-fight against Manny Pacquiao, including swimming pool workouts and underwater resistance training. Pacquiao, meanwhile, has been focusing on his fitness with challenging mountain climbs in Los Angeles. The fight, scheduled for May 2nd in Las Vegas, is predicted to be the richest in boxing history, surpassing previous pay-per-view records. With less than a month to go, both boxers are pushing themselves to peak performance for this epic clash.

Benedict Cumberbatch, known for his role as Sherlock, was challenged to a table tennis match by Chinese legend Deng Yaping during the Laureus World Sports Awards in Shanghai. Despite using a wooden spoon to level the playing field, Cumberbatch was soundly defeated by Deng, showcasing her incredible skill. The playful match, which garnered laughter from the audience, highlighted Cumberbatch's popularity in China and the global appeal of the sport. The comedic sketch ended with a dramatic final shot where Deng's smash sent the ball bouncing out of the building and back into Cumberbatch's mouth.

Manchester City players risk losing substantial bonuses, potentially £500,000 each, if they fail to qualify for the Champions League this season. Their contracts are structured to comply with financial fair play regulations, with bonuses tied to European qualification.  The team currently sits in fourth place, facing intense competition from Liverpool for a top-four spot, which guarantees Champions League entry.  A disappointing end to the season has left manager Manuel Pellegrini admitting their campaign has "disintegrated" and potentially jeopardizing their ability to attract top talent in the transfer market.

This CNN Student News episode from April 8, 2015, covers a range of topics, including the growing impact of California's drought, a flu outbreak in Chicago affecting animals, and a proposed undersea tunnel connecting Denmark and Germany.  The episode also provides a transcript and encourages viewer participation through a "Roll Call" where schools can request to be mentioned on the show. CNN Student News emphasizes its commitment to aligning with educational standards in its content creation.

Jenny's cookies, a small bakery in Hong Kong, has become the center of a black market frenzy. Tourists and locals alike queue for hours to buy the popular "little bear cookies" for $9 a tin, leading to the creation of counterfeit versions and a secondary market where people resell the cookies at a 70% markup.  This cookie craze reflects a larger global trend of food mania, where specific dishes gain immense popularity, sparking long lines and intense demand, only to fade away relatively quickly. Examples of this phenomenon include cronuts and ramen burgers, raising the question of whether the hype is worth the wait.

A 16-year-old boy from Manchester, Kevin Coulton, suffered bruising around his lips and chin after attempting the "Kylie Jenner challenge," which involves sucking on a glass to achieve a fuller pout. Coulton warns others against participating in the dangerous trend, emphasizing the pain and lasting bruising it can cause.  This craze, inspired by Kylie Jenner's fuller lips, has led to many teenagers injuring themselves while trying to emulate her look using bottles, shot glasses, and other objects.  The hashtag "#kyliejennerchallenge" has spread on social media, encouraging teens to participate in this risky behavior.

Bristol City drew 1-1 with Preston North End, extending their lead at the top of League One to eight points. A win against Bradford on Tuesday will secure promotion for Bristol City, making them the first team in the Football League to do so this season. Despite the draw, Preston manager Simon Grayson emphasizes the need for continued focus, while Bristol City manager Steve Cotterill aims to secure the League One title.  The article highlights the tension and anticipation surrounding the promotion race in League One.

President Obama pledged to ensure Israel maintains a military edge over Iran despite criticism from Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu regarding the Iran nuclear deal.  Obama emphasized his commitment to Israel's security, stating he would stand by them if attacked, and believes the deal provides clarity and a chance to address the nuclear issue.  While acknowledging concerns about Iran's trustworthiness, Obama argues that knowing their activities and preventing nuclear weapon development for a prolonged period outweighs the risks. Negotiations for a final draft of the deal continue with a deadline of June 30th.

During his appearance on The Tonight Show, Chris Christie confronted Jimmy Fallon about past weight jokes, leading Fallon to offer him ice cream as a peace offering.  Christie, however, took the entire pint for himself, refusing to share.  While Christie hasn't officially declared his candidacy for president, he stated that he won't change his personality to appeal to voters and that his current job keeps him busy.  Christie's appearance on the show highlighted his direct and unapologetic nature, characteristic of his political persona.

Manchester City's Under 18s suffered a 3-1 defeat against Chelsea in the first leg of the FA Youth Cup final, despite the presence of captain Vincent Kompany, manager Manuel Pellegrini, and head of youth development Patrick Vieira.  Chelsea took the lead early through Tammy Abraham's volley, but City quickly equalized. Abraham scored again before half-time with a acrobatic finish, and Dominic Solanke sealed the win late in the game. The result adds to criticism of City's academy, which has been overshadowed by the club's spending on established players.

A drug dealer attempted to smuggle $30,000 worth of methamphetamine by stuffing it into an Easter bunny and mailing it to Carolyn Ross in Tahlequah, Oklahoma. The package was intercepted by police dogs in Tulsa County, and upon searching the bunny, officers found two condoms filled with the drugs. Ross was arrested and confessed to knowing about the drugs in the mail, admitting she was supposed to redistribute them. Police are investigating whether this was part of a larger drug smuggling ring.

Swansea City is vying for the signature of out-of-contract Marseille striker Andre Ayew, who has also attracted interest from Newcastle, Everton, Wolfsburg, and Borussia Dortmund. Ayew, a Ghanaian international known for his World Cup performances, is keen on a Premier League move.  Separately, Swansea is interested in Schalke full-back Christian Fuchs, another player whose contract expires in the summer, as a potential replacement for Neil Taylor. Fuchs, an experienced Austrian international, has also drawn interest from Sunderland, Hull, and Fulham.

Jack Wilshere returned to action, captaining Arsenal's U21 side against Reading in a top-of-the-table clash. Despite the loss, Wilshere expressed gratitude for the fan support on social media, highlighting his desire to return to the first team.  The 23-year-old, sidelined since November with an injury, is aiming for a comeback in the season run-in and the FA Cup semi-final against Reading.  Wilshere's goal is to regain his place in the Arsenal first team and contribute to their remaining matches.

Manchester City captain Vincent Kompany revealed that his mother prevented him from joining Manchester United at 17, prioritizing his education over a football career at the time. Kompany, who eventually signed with City in 2008, credits his mother's decision for ultimately leading him to success at the Etihad Stadium. He has since become a key player for City, leading them to two Premier League titles and a growing rivalry with their Manchester rivals.  Kompany emphasizes the current parity between the two clubs, stating that Sunday's derby match will be a close contest.

Floyd Mayweather is currently in excellent shape for his upcoming fight against Manny Pacquiao, weighing in at 150.5 lbs, just 3.5 pounds over the welterweight limit with over three weeks to go. The mandatory weight check, conducted 30 days before the fight, ensures boxers don't drastically cut weight at the last minute, reducing the risk of brain damage. Both Mayweather and Pacquiao are well within the weight limit requirements, and Mayweather is emphasizing the importance of rest and recovery through cryotherapy treatments. The highly anticipated "Fight of the Century" is scheduled for May 2nd at the MGM Grand in Las Vegas.

Australia's largest venomous snake, a four-meter long king cobra named Raja, yielded almost 500 milligrams of venom at the Australian Reptile Park. This is a record amount, 40 times the venom of a brown snake and enough to kill an elephant. Veteran handler Billy Collett, who has been milking snakes for a decade, extracted the venom safely and it will be distributed to research institutes across the country. The milking also allowed for the safe weighing and assessment of Raja, who was last assessed four years ago.

Joe Root scored an impressive 182 runs against the West Indies in the second Test in Grenada, but was denied a double century due to a careless run out by James Anderson.  Anderson failed to run his bat into the ground after Root called for the second run, allowing Jason Holder to take a catch and run Root out. Despite his frustration, Root and Anderson made up after the incident, and England held a 165-run lead going into the second innings. Root's masterful batting performance was overshadowed by the unfortunate run out.

An old hoax video of a surfer riding a wave in front of the Sydney Opera House resurfaced online, sparking confusion and amusement. Despite heavy storms in Sydney, experts confirmed the video's impossibility, stating that ocean swells can't break that far inside the harbor.  The video, originally posted in 2012, features numerous inconsistencies, including the appearance of rocks around the Opera House and a cardboard-like figure in the final shot.  The video's resurgence was fueled by popular Sydney radio duo Fitzy and Wippa, who shared it on Twitter, leading to widespread sharing and discussion.

Manny Pacquiao trained in the open air at Griffith Park in Los Angeles, preparing for his upcoming fight against Floyd Mayweather.  He focused on improving his stamina, core strength, and footwork, shadow boxing for onlookers and jogging with his dog, Pacman.  This outdoor session comes ahead of Pacquiao's May 2nd showdown in Las Vegas, where he will face the undefeated Mayweather in a highly anticipated bout.  Pacquiao has shown confidence in his preparation, recently unveiling a colorful mouthguard he will use during the fight.

Three dogs - Sky the Husky, Sadie and Marshall the Springer Spaniels - formed a unique musical ensemble for their owner in Bahama, North Carolina. Sadie began with barks, prompting Sky to join with a melodic howl, which in turn inspired Sadie to howl as well. The trio, joined by Marshall's deep growls, harmonized together, lifting their noses to the sky and howling in unison. The performance ended abruptly when their owner called for quiet, leaving the dogs surprised and seemingly disappointed.

West Ham is targeting Real Madrid's loan striker Javier Hernandez for their summer transfer campaign. Hernandez, known as "the Little Pea," will return to Manchester United at the end of the season but is not in Louis van Gaal's plans and could be sold for around £7 million.  While other Premier League clubs may be interested, West Ham is primarily focused on younger players and are also pursuing Sampdoria's Pedro Obiang, FC Sion's Moussa Konate, and Basel's Fabian Schar.

A single-decker coach caught fire on the A2 slip road to the M25 near Dartford, Kent, this morning. The driver was the only occupant and escaped unharmed. Fire crews battled the blaze for an hour, closing the slip road while they extinguished the fire that started in the engine compartment and spread to the coach's body.  The coach was completely destroyed, and the slip road remains closed as wreckage is removed, causing traffic delays in the area.

Brazil's Dunas Arena in Natal, built for the 2014 World Cup, is up for sale due to financial difficulties faced by its owner, Grupo OAS. The company, burdened by a corruption scandal and economic downturn, is divesting itself of stadium assets to focus on core construction. OAS invested heavily in both Dunas Arena and Fonte Nova Arena in Salvador, which hosted World Cup matches, but struggles to access financing and declining revenue have forced the sale. This news adds to a string of post-World Cup challenges for Brazilian football, including dwindling fan attendance and stadium closures.

The Director of Public Prosecutions, Alison Saunders, faced mounting criticism for her decision not to prosecute Lord Janner on child abuse allegations.  It was revealed that Saunders consulted with a barrister from the same chambers as Lord Janner's son, raising concerns about potential bias. While Saunders justified her decision based on Lord Janner's poor health due to Alzheimer's, she admitted he should have been charged previously. The controversy sparked calls for a judicial review and fueled accusations of an establishment cover-up, leading to demands for Saunders' resignation.

Charlie Austin, QPR's prolific striker with 17 goals this season, is being considered for a surprise England call-up due to Harry Kane's absence with the Under-21 team.  England manager Roy Hodgson has been impressed with Austin's consistent performances despite QPR's relegation battle, and the striker is now a contender for the upcoming friendly against Republic of Ireland and the Euro 2016 qualifier in Slovenia. Hodgson is evaluating his attacking options after Theo Walcott's trial as a forward was unsuccessful. Austin's future at QPR is uncertain as contract talks have been postponed until the end of the season.

The highly anticipated return of Game of Thrones has led to a surge in internet piracy, with over seven million illegal downloads of past seasons between February and April 2015. This represents a 45% increase compared to the same period in 2014, making Game of Thrones the most illegally downloaded show globally.  Factors like delayed releases in certain countries and lack of global access contribute to piracy, prompting HBO to address the issue by simultaneously airing the new season in 170 countries and launching its streaming service HBO Now.  Despite these efforts, Irdeto, an anti-piracy firm, acknowledges that piracy is a persistent challenge requiring continuous management to protect business models and ensure the availability of quality content.

Patrice Evra, former Manchester United left back, revealed that Sir Alex Ferguson predicted he would become a great coach.  Before Ferguson's departure in 2013, he told Evra and Ryan Giggs they had the potential for successful managerial careers. Evra has already begun pursuing coaching qualifications, obtaining some badges while still at Manchester United. He now aims to complete his coaching diploma and explore managerial opportunities in the future.

A recent study by the University of Southern California identified a gene, Nox3, located in the inner ear, that significantly influences an individual's susceptibility to noise-induced hearing loss. This breakthrough discovery, based on a genome-wide association study in mice, suggests that genetic predisposition plays a crucial role in determining hearing vulnerability to loud noise.  The findings could empower individuals with a higher genetic risk to proactively take protective measures against noise exposure and prevent hearing loss.  This research paves the way for future studies exploring targeted interventions and personalized preventive strategies for noise-induced hearing loss.

Susie Clark from Arkansas found a 3.69-carat white diamond at Crater of Diamonds State Park, the largest found there since 2014.  Clark, who prayed for a diamond before her search, named the gem "Hallelujah Diamond" after spotting it protruding from plowed dirt. This discovery is attributed to recent rain and plowing which bring diamonds closer to the surface. Crater of Diamonds State Park is the only publicly accessible diamond-bearing site in the United States.

Brett Anthony O'Connor, a former head of child safety at Education Queensland, has been denied a suppression order that would have prevented media from naming him during his trial for child sex offences. O'Connor, who was charged with assaulting two 12-year-old boys over 25 years ago, was facing ten counts of sex offences, including indecent assault and assault involving sexual intercourse with a child under 16.  While the magistrate refused to suppress his name, details of the victims and any witnesses will be kept confidential. The case has been adjourned to the Campbelltown court on June 10th.

Former Arsenal goalkeeper David Seaman considers his iconic save against Sheffield United in the 2003 FA Cup semi-final to be the best of his career, surpassing even his penalty heroics at Euro 96. The save, which denied Sheffield United an equalizer at Old Trafford, was made during Seaman's 1,000th competitive appearance for Arsenal and England.  Seaman, who won the FA Cup with Arsenal that year, warns his former team against underestimating Reading in their current FA Cup semi-final clash. He emphasizes the unpredictable nature of football and urges Arsenal not to take their opponents lightly.

Joel Burger and Ashley King, a couple who have been known as "Burger-King" since childhood, are having their wedding expenses covered by the fast-food chain. Burger King contacted the engaged couple via Skype and offered to pay for their July 17th wedding in Jacksonville, along with providing personalized gifts like Burger King crowns and Mason jars. The couple, who announced their engagement with a photo next to a local Burger King sign, expressed their astonishment and gratitude for the generous offer. Burger King spokesman Eric Hirschhorn stated they wanted to create a "whopper of a wedding" that was unique to the couple.

A mystery man, found in Gniezno, Poland, in February, is unable to recall his identity or origin.  He speaks English, along with a few Polish and other languages, but his accent remains unidentified.  The man, described as 6ft3in tall and approximately 35 years old, was discovered disoriented and wandering into traffic, prompting a call to the ambulance. Polish authorities are seeking public assistance in identifying him and are working with experts to determine his accent and possible nationality.

Raheem Sterling has rejected a lucrative £100,000-a-week contract offer to stay at Liverpool, indicating he is seeking a move away from the club. Although previously willing to sign for significantly less, Sterling's form has dipped this season, scoring only six goals compared to last year's impressive tally. Despite being a key player for Liverpool, his lack of goals and declining efficiency have contributed to his decision to leave.  His current contract situation leaves Liverpool facing the possibility of losing one of their star players.

A large fire broke out in the afternoon on Wednesday at a building in Flint, Michigan, that formerly housed a strip club called The Body Shop.  The fire caused significant damage to the structure and some nearby power lines, but no other buildings were affected.  While the building was not vacant, the cause of the fire is still under investigation. Local resident Terry Rauh mentioned that a previous fire had occurred at the same location.

Manchester United winger Ashley Young played a crucial role in his team's 4-1 victory over Manchester City, scoring one goal and assisting on two more. Young highlighted the team's focus on silencing the boisterous City fans, describing the home crowd as the loudest he'd ever heard.  He expressed the team's newfound confidence, believing they can win every remaining match and finish the season strongly. Young attributed the win to their ability to maintain control of the game after taking a 3-1 lead.

A recent survey by the British Chiropractic Association revealed a sharp increase in back and neck pain among young people, with nearly half of those under 30 reporting constant pain. Experts attribute this surge to increasingly sedentary lifestyles, excessive mobile phone use, and poor posture while using devices.  As gadgets become smaller, users lean forward more, putting strain on the neck and vertebrae.  The survey also highlighted the prevalence of prolonged sitting in modern life, with the average Briton spending ten hours a day in a seated position.

David Villa, who played for Atletico Madrid last season, showed his continued support for the club by cheering them on during their Champions League quarter-final match against Real Madrid. Despite not securing a regular starting position at Atletico, Villa has been enjoying his time with New York City FC, serving as club captain and scoring in their recent victory.  The Champions League match ended in a 0-0 draw, with Gareth Bale missing a crucial opportunity for Real Madrid. Villa will be joined by former England star Frank Lampard at New York City FC later this season.

A father, Casey Levi, challenged his son Sam to eat a California roll for a $10 prize, but Sam refused due to his strong dislike of sushi.  His younger sister, Charlie, stepped up to the challenge and successfully ate the roll, winning the prize money.  The video of the "sushi challenge" went viral, with viewers finding the children's reactions both cute and relatable.  Many commenters expressed sympathy for Sam's aversion to sushi.

A six-year-old girl, Nurhayada Sofia, tragically died after falling five floors from an escalator at a Malaysian shopping mall.  CCTV footage shows the girl playing with the handrail at the top of the escalator before being dragged over the side and vanishing. Her distraught mother, who was on the phone with her husband at the time, is said to be inconsolable. An investigation has been launched to determine if the mall breached safety regulations.

Celta Vigo forward Fabian Orellana has been suspended for one match after throwing a clump of grass at Barcelona midfielder Sergio Busquets during their 1-0 loss. The incident occurred as Orellana grew frustrated with Busquets' time-wasting tactics, prompting a red card from referee Inaki Vicandi Garrido. Despite Busquets theatrically reacting to the grass striking his neck, the referee deemed Orellana's actions worthy of immediate dismissal. Orellana will miss Celta Vigo's next game against Granada.

In a series of exciting Serie A matches, Fiorentina defeated Sampdoria 2-0, thanks to goals from Alessandro Diamanti and Mohamed Salah, propelling them into fourth place. Roma secured their first home victory in over four months with a 1-0 win against Napoli, courtesy of Miralem Pjanic's goal. Juventus remained undefeated at the top of the table with a 2-0 victory over Empoli, fueled by Carlos Tevez's powerful free kick and Roberto Pereyra's late goal. AC Milan finally broke their away win drought, edging past Palermo 2-1, while Inter Milan drew 1-1 with struggling Parma.

Adele Berry, a Vodafone customer, was subjected to unwanted and inappropriate text messages from a call centre worker in South Africa after contacting the company about a billing issue. The worker, who identified himself as "Miguel", sent numerous messages referring to himself as her "future hubby" and expressing romantic interest, including photos of himself and kissing emoticons.  Vodafone has suspended the call handler pending a full investigation, stating they take such allegations seriously. Berry expressed her fear and distress over the persistent messages, feeling harassed by an unknown individual.

During the eight-year conflict in Afghanistan, the British Army expended a staggering 46 million bullets at a cost of £200 million. This extensive ammunition usage, including 27 million 5.56mm rounds, 2 million 9mm rounds, and 80,000 105mm artillery shells, highlights the intense nature of the fighting against the Taliban.  Military veterans emphasize that the sheer volume of ammunition used demonstrates the constant threat faced by British troops and the close-quarters combat they endured. The conflict resulted in the loss of 453 British lives.

In a heartbreaking turn of events, Mr. Zhang, a man in his 50s, died in a road accident in Dezhou City, China, eight months after his younger brother was killed at the same spot. Mr. Zhang was riding his motorbike when a car hit him, throwing him from the vehicle. The car fled the scene without stopping, and Mr. Zhang later succumbed to his injuries in the hospital.  Police are investigating the driver, who was using a fake registration plate, and are reminding motorists to be cautious at junctions.

Firefighter Cody Knecht used a clever tactic to rescue six ducklings trapped in a storm drain in Louisiana. He lured them out with a duck quacking ringtone, carefully retrieving them one by one with a pole and his hands. It took Knecht 90 minutes to capture the first four ducklings, and another hour for the remaining two to calm down. All six ducklings were safely reunited with their mother on a nearby canal.

An 11-year-old Indian boy named Shiva died after being allegedly beaten severely by his headmaster, Lalit Verma, for stealing a pencil and eraser.  The beating was so brutal that Shiva suffered internal bleeding and vomited blood before dying at the hospital.  Verma has been arrested on charges of culpable homicide and admitted to slapping Shiva during questioning. This tragic incident highlights a disturbing trend of corporal punishment leading to death in Indian schools.

Charlie Austin, QPR's top scorer with 15 goals this season, shares a close friendship with club captain Joey Barton. Their bond began after a rocky start, as Barton initially ignored Austin on his first day at the club due to a misunderstanding. The two men quickly became close friends, bonding over dinner and supporting each other through QPR's relegation battle. Austin, currently fourth in the Premier League scoring charts, aims to secure crucial points against West Brom and Aston Villa to help his team avoid relegation.

A nine-year-old boy in China was severely physically abused by his adoptive mother, Li, who beat him with skipping ropes, metal rods, and water pipes for not knowing the story of Pinocchio.  School teachers discovered the abuse six months prior but initially assumed it was a isolated incident.  The boy's injuries, which included scars, cuts, and bruising, were shared online, prompting Li's arrest on child abuse charges. The boy is now in the care of his biological parents and receiving psychological support.

A secret police intelligence report from 1964 revealed that an unnamed popular 1960s band frequented the same paedophile brothel in Battersea, South West London as disgraced BBC star Jimmy Savile. While it is unclear if the band and Savile were present at the same time, both have been linked to the notorious property, with Savile confirmed as a regular visitor in the 1970s.  This information came to light in 2013 when Detective Superintendent David Grey, who led Operation Yewtree, began investigating the brothel after a woman reported being abused by Savile at the same location in the 1970s. Despite the revelation, police stated that the information alone did not provide grounds for further investigation at the time

During White House Take Our Daughters and Sons to Work Day, Michelle Obama was complimented by a young girl named Anya Brodie on her youthful appearance.  When Obama revealed her age was 51, Brodie exclaimed, "You look too young!" The First Lady was touched by the compliment and invited Brodie on stage for a hug.  The event brought together children of White House employees, Boys and Girls Club members, and kids from D.C Child and Family Services for a question-and-answer session.

Georginio Wijnaldum, midfielder for PSV Eindhoven, has expressed his desire to play for a top club in a major European league, fueling speculation of a summer move to the Premier League.  The 24-year-old, who has been linked with Manchester United, Arsenal, and Newcastle, has spoken positively about the English league and life in Newcastle, citing the strong competition and passionate fanbase as attractive factors. While currently happy at PSV, Wijnaldum believes his talent would be better utilized in a more challenging environment like the Premier League. His ambition to test himself against top-tier competition suggests he is eager to take the next step in his career.

The British Army's 77 Brigade, a specialized unit tasked with countering Islamic State's online influence, is facing difficulties recruiting the necessary technical expertise. Despite initial plans to have 2,000 personnel, the brigade will only have 454 soldiers and reservists upon full operation. Brigadier Alastair Aitken, the brigade commander, acknowledges the struggle to attract "computer whizzkids" skilled in social media, finance, and counter-criminality, suggesting potential changes to recruitment terms to appeal to civilian talent.  The recruitment shortfall and lack of clarity regarding roles have reportedly created a "mutinous atmosphere" within the brigade.

A farmer in China has a unique attraction on his farm: a six-legged goat kid born with four forelegs and two hind legs.  The healthy and playful kid, nicknamed the "spider goat" online, is growing rapidly and has an extraordinary appetite. The farmer, Xiao Qibin, is unsure what caused the mutation and plans to let the goat develop naturally. Despite initial concerns, the six-legged kid is thriving and is now one of the most playful goats on the farm.

In separate incidents in Melbourne, a garbage truck driver died after being pinned by his own vehicle in Footscray, while another man died and two others are critically injured following a car crash in Launching Place. The garbage truck driver was collecting bins when the truck rolled forward, crushing him against a pole.  The car crash occurred when the driver lost control, causing the vehicle to roll over after hitting trees.  Both incidents are under investigation.

Scott Stephenson, a 19-year-old from South Shields, is facing jail time for stealing a mobile phone from a man who was dying of hypothermia. Stephenson and his friend, Dale Walker, found the unconscious man and stole his iPhone instead of calling for help. Stephenson was due to be sentenced but failed to appear in court, leading to a warrant for his arrest and an outburst outside the court where he made rude gestures and shouted.  He has now been re-bailed pending a sentencing date.

Harry Redknapp, the former Tottenham manager, was subjected to coin-throwing and verbal abuse from a group of Arsenal fans after the Gunners' win over Liverpool. Despite earlier positive interactions with supporters, Redknapp was targeted while stuck in traffic, highlighting the issue of a small minority spoiling the experience for the majority. Redknapp, who is unpopular with Arsenal fans due to his time at Tottenham, emphasized the negativity he has experienced as a manager, including personal and horrific abuse directed at him and his family.  He contrasted this with the generally positive atmosphere of football, expressing his appreciation for the majority of fans who create a welcoming environment.

Arsenal defender Per Mertesacker attributes the team's recent improvement to a renewed emphasis on "arguing culture" within the team.  Following embarrassing defeats last season, Mertesacker believes honest, even heated, confrontations helped identify problems and foster a more united approach.  He argues that this open communication, though sometimes uncomfortable, is crucial for a team's development and success.  This season, Arsenal's renewed intensity and focus have propelled them to a strong position in the Premier League title race.

A 19-year-old woman, Miranda Ashley Calvin, was arrested in Oregon for the manslaughter of 49-year-old Jerrie Ann Hornig, who was struck by Calvin's car and left at the scene. Police were able to locate Calvin with the help of public tips and a witness who saw a fleeing white Mercedes Benz matching the description of Calvin's vehicle.  Calvin has been booked into the Multnomah County Detention Center on charges of manslaughter, felony hit-and-run, and reckless driving. Hornig's son, Maxwell, expressed his grief on Facebook, remembering his mother and the tragic circumstances of her death.

Martin Skrtel has firmly stated that he will not be leaving Liverpool, despite rumors linking him with moves to Wolfsburg and Napoli. The Slovakian defender, who has made over 200 Premier League appearances for the Reds, is in talks to extend his contract which expires in 2016.  His agent confirmed that Skrtel has no other offers on the table and that rumors of interest from Wolfsburg are false.  The 30-year-old is committed to Liverpool despite the club's disappointing season.

Britney E. Montenegro, a 20-year-old student from New York, was arrested in Orlando, Florida, early Saturday morning for disorderly conduct after a fight involving multiple people. The altercation started inside a bar and spilled onto the streets, where Montenegro allegedly charged at another woman. Police used an arm-bar takedown technique to restrain her, and while she sustained a minor abrasion, she was covered in another person's blood. Montenegro was charged and taken into custody.

Lucas Moura, a PSG playmaker from Brazil, expressed admiration for Lionel Messi, calling him his idol and the best player in the world. Despite the historical rivalry between Brazil and Argentina, Moura acknowledges Messi's exceptional talent and anticipates a challenging match against Barcelona in the Champions League.  Moura believes stopping Messi will be crucial for PSG's success, recognizing that victory against the La Liga giants will be a hard-fought battle.  The two teams face off first at the Parc des Princes on Wednesday and then at the Nou Camp on April 21st.

Eligah Christian, 49, led Alaska State Troopers on a high-speed chase in a motorhome after attempting to evade arrest on a $100,000 warrant for charges including scheming to defraud and issuing bad checks. Christian drove recklessly, driving through Wasilla near Sarah Palin's home before crashing into patrol cars while trying to turn around after spike strips were deployed.  He was ultimately apprehended and faces additional charges of felony reckless driving and criminal mischief. Christian remains jailed.

The iconic blue "Yankees" letters that once illuminated Yankee Stadium failed to sell at a Sotheby's auction, despite pre-sale estimates of $300,000 to $600,000.  Owned by Hall of Famer Reggie Jackson, who purchased them in 2008, the letters drew only a single, unsuccessful bid from a Sotheby's employee. Jackson had hoped a new owner would cherish the landmark signage, but ultimately they were unsold. Interestingly, a vintage Mets bullpen car fetched a surprising $112,500, exceeding its own estimated value.

Bob Shannon, a boxing trainer known for his work with Ricky Hatton and his brother Matthew, has been charged with sexually assaulting a girl under the age of 16. The charge stems from a report filed last June, and Shannon is scheduled to appear in court on April 22nd, 2015. Shannon was briefly Hatton's trainer during his ill-fated 2012 comeback attempt, a period marked by a loss to Vyacheslav Senchenko.  Prior to this, Shannon had guided Matthew Hatton to a world title shot and trained several other fighters who secured British and European titles.

Tottenham Hotspur is in negotiations with midfielder Nabil Bentaleb for a new, improved contract to keep him at the club.  His current deal expires in 2018, but Tottenham is keen to secure his future sooner rather than later due to his impressive performances and interest from other European clubs. The new contract is expected to significantly increase Bentaleb's wages, potentially to around £35,000 per week. This move aims to reward his progress and prevent him from joining another team.

Kenny Dalglish argues that Harry Kane should focus on the senior England squad instead of playing for the Under-21 team this summer. He believes Kane's recent performance against Italy's strong defense proves he is ready for the senior level and that playing with the younger team would only tire him out. Dalglish suggests that the Under-21s should focus on developing players for the senior team, rather than winning tournaments, and Kane should be prioritized for the 2016 European Championships. Tottenham will likely support this decision, hoping to keep their star striker fresh.

Orlando City defeated the Portland Timbers 2-0 at Providence Park thanks to goals from Cyle Larin and Kaka. Larin put Orlando ahead in the first half with a well-placed chest shot, while Kaka sealed the victory with a penalty in the closing minutes after the Timbers goalkeeper fouled a substitute. Despite Darlington Nagbe's efforts, the Timbers struggled to create opportunities against a solid Orlando defense. The win moved Orlando City into third place in the table.

An airport worker at LaGuardia Airport in New York went viral after NBC journalist Jeff Rossen filmed him doing push-ups on the runway after loading a plane. The worker, wearing his high-visibility gear, completed 12 push-ups without stopping, showcasing his dedication to fitness. Rossen, impressed by the worker's dedication, shared the video on social media, praising him as the "hardest working man at LaGuardia." The video quickly garnered attention online, with many viewers commending the worker's commitment to staying fit even during his job.

Dirk Kuyt is returning to his former club, Feyenoord, after a successful career with Liverpool, Fenerbahce, and the Netherlands national team.  He signed a one-year contract but both he and the club hope it will extend much longer. Kuyt, a fan favorite from his first stint at Feyenoord from 2003-2006, is eager to contribute to the team's success. The 34-year-old forward is considered fit and driven to make a positive impact at De Kuip.

A truck crash on Interstate 93 in Boston caused major delays for commuters this morning. The collision between a box truck and a car resulted in both drivers being hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries.  To prevent potential danger to passing trains, shuttle buses replaced southbound train service, leading to long lines and further delays.  Even after the truck was removed, traffic congestion persisted in both directions.

Liverpool goalkeeper Simon Mignolet returned to England after representing Belgium in Euro 2016 qualifiers, despite not playing in either match due to Thibaut Courtois being preferred.  Mignolet, who posted pictures of himself in a plane cockpit on Facebook, is now back with Liverpool ahead of their crucial match against Arsenal.  The 27-year-old goalkeeper has overcome early-season struggles to become a vital player in Liverpool's recent resurgence, recently achieving six consecutive Premier League clean sheets away from home. Mignolet credits his mental strength for helping him through the difficult period and emphasizes his continuous desire to learn and improve.

The new Rangers board, led by Paul Murray and John Gilligan, held their first meeting with Mike Ashley and his Sports Direct representatives to discuss the club's future.  Ashley, who owns 8.92% of Rangers and has a strong financial grip on the club through loans and merchandising rights, is hoping for an amicable resolution. The new board, which ousted Ashley allies from the previous board, is currently studying existing contracts and seeking alternative funding sources, rejecting a second loan from Sports Direct. The board hopes to find a way forward that benefits the club and its supporters.

Liverpool striker Daniel Sturridge was left in stitches by comedian Darren Farley's impressive impersonation of club legend Michael Owen during a promotional video for BT Sport's FA Cup coverage. Farley, known for his YouTube videos mimicking various Anfield stars, flawlessly captured Owen's mannerisms, leaving Sturridge in hysterics. While the comedian's Michael Owen impression was spot-on, his attempt at Sturridge's famous goal celebration dance was less successful.  Sturridge himself is facing a race against time to overcome a hip injury and potentially feature in the semi-final against Aston Villa.

Clark Elmore, convicted of raping and killing his 14-year-old stepdaughter in 1995, had his conviction and death sentence upheld by a federal appeals court. The 9th US Circuit Court of Appeals agreed with the Washington Supreme Court's decision, rejecting Elmore's claims of constitutional violations during his trial. Despite the ruling, Elmore will not face execution immediately due to a moratorium on the death penalty in Washington state. Although he can still appeal to the US Supreme Court, he is currently serving a life sentence on death row.

Swansea City are actively seeking a proven striker for the upcoming season and have identified several targets, including Rudy Gestede of Blackburn Rovers.  Blackburn values Gestede at £7 million, but Swansea faces competition from other Premier League clubs for the striker's signature.  The Swans are also interested in Aleksandar Mitrovic of Anderlecht and Obbi Oulare of Club Brugge, while they are considering signing a left-back as well.  Meanwhile, Blackburn is facing financial constraints and may need to sell players like Jordan Rhodes to balance their books.

In southern Minnesota, farmer Daniel Weness reported the theft of approximately $70,000 worth of bull semen from his unlocked barn between April 1st and 7th. The stolen semen, valued between $300 and $1,500 per vial, was housed in a canister worth $500.  Authorities believe the theft occurred due to a market for bull semen, which helps farmers reduce transportation costs by enabling artificial insemination.  The semen, which can last for many years, is often sought after for its genetic qualities and the potential to increase herd diversity.

Wesley Burton, a beloved radio host at KPFA in Berkeley, was killed in a hit-and-run collision early Saturday morning. The 36-year-old was driving home from work when a white Dodge Charger struck his car, and the driver fled the scene on foot. Burton, a father of three, had dedicated 20 years to KPFA and was passionate about being a present father figure, having grown up without one himself. Police are actively investigating the incident and offering a $10,000 reward for information leading to an arrest.

Serge Gnabry, an Arsenal midfielder, is nearing a return to the first team after over a year out due to a serious knee injury. The 19-year-old impressed in the previous season but has been sidelined since March 2014.  Gnabry recently played 90 minutes for Arsenal's Under 21s against Reading and is feeling confident in his recovery. He expressed his excitement about playing again and looking forward to future matches.

Bastian Schweinsteiger, Bayern Munich's midfield captain, is doubtful for their German Cup quarter-final against Bayer Leverkusen due to an ankle injury sustained during their recent Bundesliga win against Borussia Dortmund. While the injury is not deemed serious, Schweinsteiger missed training on Monday and his participation in the match remains uncertain.  Adding to Bayern's injury woes, reserve goalkeeper Tom Starke was ruled out for four weeks with an ankle ligament tear, further depleting their squad.  Bayern, already missing several key players, including Arjen Robben, David Alaba, Holger Badstuber, and Franck Ribery, will hope for Schweinsteiger's recovery as they pursue a potential treble this season.

Manchester City unveiled a 64-meter mural at their Academy Stadium depicting the positive influence of football on lives around the world. The mural, designed by Manchester-based artists Outhouse using images from young City fans, features scenes of football played globally and places the City Football Academy at its center.  City stars, including Vincent Kompany and Gael Clichy, joined over 100 local schoolchildren for a street party to unveil the mural, emphasizing the club's commitment to community engagement. This initiative aligns with City's "Cityzens Giving" charitable program, which invests in extending the positive impact of football to communities in need worldwide.

Two 12-13 year old boys are being sought by Thames Valley Police after they sprayed a 12-year-old boy with WD40 in an underpass and threatened to set him alight.  The attack, which occurred on Easter Monday afternoon, was unprovoked and the victim suffered minor injuries but did not require hospitalization. Police have released descriptions of the suspects and are urging anyone with information to come forward, especially a woman and her partner who may have witnessed the incident. The investigation is ongoing to bring the perpetrators to justice.

Arsenal are in advanced talks to sign 16-year-old Argentine prodigy Maxi Romero for £4.5 million. Dubbed the "next Lionel Messi," Romero would stay on loan at his current club, Velez Sarsfield, for at least two more seasons before joining the Gunners. The deal comes after Arsenal manager Arsene Wenger was highly impressed by Romero during meetings with his agent.  This signing reflects Arsenal's ongoing commitment to developing young talent, following their acquisition of Krystian Bielik from Legia Warsaw earlier this year.

Dani Alves's agent, Dinorah Santana, confirmed Barcelona has offered the veteran full-back a new contract despite his expiring deal this summer.  Several top European clubs, including Manchester United and Liverpool, have been monitoring Alves's situation. Although Santana received an offer, she remains noncommittal on its details and anticipates further negotiations with Barcelona.

Two school bus drivers, Steve and Sarah Nick, were arrested after police discovered a massive arsenal of weapons, including a 50-caliber machine gun and 17,000 rounds of ammunition, hidden in a basement bunker at their home. The couple is accused of stealing over $50,000 from Sarah's mother's bank account to fund their weapons acquisition, and they also face charges related to a short-barreled rifle found on the property. Authorities believe the Nicks had ties to the Southeast Michigan Volunteer Militia, a group focused on disaster preparedness.  The couple has been released on bail and will appear in court on Thursday.
